# Test fixtures

`orientation_6.jpg` — synthesized (not a camera photo) tiny JPEG, 2362 bytes,
generated by `make_orientation_6.py` (PIL 10.2.0 + piexif 1.1.3). Regenerate
with:

```bash
pip install --target /path/to/pylibs piexif   # or: python3 -m pip install piexif
PYTHONPATH=/path/to/pylibs python3 culler-core/tests/fixtures/make_orientation_6.py
```

Why synthesized: this build environment has no camera source and no
`exiftool`/ImageMagick (`magick`) binary to shoot or verify a real portrait
JPEG against, so a real-photo fixture (the plan's original Step 0) was not
obtainable here. PIL + piexif are available and produce genuine, independently
verifiable EXIF bytes, so the fixture is generated deterministically by the
committed script instead of hand-copied from a device.

Guaranteed properties (verified independently of the Rust code under test —
see below):

- Main image stored **landscape**, 120x80 (w > h), non-uniform RGB gradient
  content (not a solid color).
- EXIF IFD0 `Orientation = 6` (the "rotate 90 CW to display" code): decoding
  the raw stored pixels gives a landscape (w > h) buffer; applying
  orientation 6 must yield a portrait (h > w) buffer.
- EXIF IFD1 carries a real embedded JPEG thumbnail (`JPEGInterchangeFormat` /
  `JPEGInterchangeFormatLength` tags 513/514 point at genuine JPEG bytes
  inside the EXIF blob), also stored **landscape**, 30x20, gradient content —
  so orienting the thumbnail with the primary image's Orientation=6 also
  yields a portrait result. Task 6 (embedded-thumbnail decode) depends on
  this thumbnail existing and being a valid, decodable JPEG.

Independent verification performed when the fixture was generated (PIL/piexif,
no dependency on culler-core's own decode/EXIF code):

```
$ python3 -c "from PIL import Image; im = Image.open('orientation_6.jpg'); \
    print(im.size); print(dict(im.getexif()).get(274))"
(120, 80)
6

$ PYTHONPATH=.../pylibs python3 -c "import piexif; d = piexif.load('orientation_6.jpg'); \
    print(len(d['thumbnail']))"
752
```

(Full session output, including the thumbnail's own decoded size (30, 20) and
the IFD1 tag values, is recorded in the Task 5 implementation report.)

Used by `decode.rs`'s `decode_applies_exif_orientation` test (Task 5) and the
embedded-thumbnail test (Task 6).
